merged_events.event_description: The Consulate General at Saigon was raised to Legation status, establishing diplomatic relations with the State of Viet Nam, with Edmund A. Gullion as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on February 17, 1950, when the Consulate General at Saigon was raised to Legation status with Edmund A. Gullion as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Legation in Saigon was raised to Embassy status, following a joint announcement by the Governments of the United States and Vietnam on June 6, 1952.

evidence.quote: The United States Legation in Saigon was raised to Embassy status on June 24, 1952, when Ambassador Donald R. Heath received confirmation of his appointment from the United States Senate. This followed a joint announcement by the Governments of the United States and Vietnam to this effect on June 6, 1952.

merged_events.event_description: The United States closed the Embassy in Saigon and evacuated all Embassy personnel, just prior to the surrender of South Vietnam to North Vietnamese forces.

evidence.quote: The United States closed the Embassy in Saigon and evacuated all Embassy personnel on April 29, 1975, just prior to the surrender of South Vietnam to North Vietnamese forces.

merged_events.event_description: The United States opened a Liaison Office in Hanoi, establishing an informal diplomatic presence prior to the full resumption of relations.

evidence.quote: The United States opened a Liaison Office in Hanoi on January 28, 1995.

merged_events.event_description: Embassy Hanoi opened following the reestablishment of diplomatic relations on August 5, 1995, with L. Desaix Anderson serving as Chargé d'Affaires a.i.

evidence.quote: The United States and Vietnam reestablished diplomatic relations on August 5, 1995 during Secretary of State Warren Christopher's visit to Vietnam, when he and Vietnam's Foreign Minister signed documents agreeing to do so and to open embassies in Hanoi and Washington. The opening of Embassy Hanoi took place on August 6.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy in Vietnam established; Desaix Anderson presented credentials as the first Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, marking the opening of diplomatic relations at the embassy level.

evidence.quote: L Desaix Anderson, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1995-08-26. Left post: 1997-05-07.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1950-02-17) and status (Legation).
Exact match on date (1975-04-29) and status (None). Embassy closed and personnel evacuated.
Exact match on date (1995-01-28) and status (Liaison). Liaison Office opened in Hanoi.

CSV date 1995-08-06 matches RDCR Event 4 (Embassy Hanoi opened August 6). Event 5 from POCOM gives 1995-08-26, which is the credential presentation date for the Chargé d'Affaires, a later personnel event. RDCR is the primary source and confirms August 6 as the institutional opening date. Event 4 has a citation error flag, but the quoted evidence text clearly supports the August 6 date.

unsupported_in_sources.notes: No extracted event covers the 1907-12-09 Consulate establishment. The RDCR narrative for Vietnam appears to begin with the 1950 establishment of diplomatic relations with the State of Viet Nam, and POCOM records do not cover this early consular presence. This may reflect information from other historical sources not included in this pipeline (e.g., records of French Indochina-era consular posts).

discrepancies.reasoning: The extracted event used June 6, 1952 as the date, but the RDCR evidence text it cites actually states: 'The United States Legation in Saigon was raised to Embassy status on June 24, 1952, when Ambassador Donald R. Heath received confirmation of his appointment from the United States Senate. This followed a joint announcement by the Governments of the United States and Vietnam to this effect on June 6, 1952.' The extraction incorrectly selected the June 6 announcement date rather than the June 24 institutional elevation date. The CSV's 1952-06-24 correctly reflects the RDCR source text for when the Legation was actually raised to Embassy status.
